Just now

Photo by Shantanu KumarRecently, I passed the AWS Certified AI Practitioner exam. It was my first exam from the foundational group. Previously, I did a few associate and specialty certifications, such as AWS Certified Developer Associate.
I was curious to know more about the AI domain from a non-developer perspective. It took me a few months to prepare for it. The exam is not as complex as the exams from the other groups. Yet it teaches something new.
The exams in the foundational group check basic understanding of AI and cloud technologies and are a perfect choice for business analysts and product managers.
As a developer, I work with different types of AI daily. Nevertheless, I learned a few new things while preparing for the certification.
Zero-shot prompt vs. few-shot prompt
A zero-shot prompt is a prompt engineering technique in which an AI is asked to perform a task without any examples. In that case, LLM needs to figure out on its own how to address the request.
This can sound like a generic request similar to " What is the weather in Dublin tomorrow?”. And indeed it is. But what can be the benefits of using it?
